steps.title: Write standard form
steps.content:
Move $t$ to the left side so the equation is a quadratic equal to $0$.
$$3x^2-6x-t=0$$
Here $a=3$, $b=-6$, and $c=-t$.

steps.title: Use the discriminant
steps.content:
A quadratic has no real solutions when the discriminant is negative.
$$b^2-4ac<0$$
$$(-6)^2-4(3)(-t)<0$$
$$36+12t<0$$
$$t<-3$$

steps.title: Pick a valid value
steps.content: Among the choices, only $-4$ is less than $-3$, so it makes the discriminant negative. The answer is A.
